A startling move to overturn the
plans of water extensions prepared
by the late water commissioner,
Arvid ReuterdahI, approved by Pro-
fessor William n. Burr, and order
ed built by the city council, has
developed at the city hall, in the
executive department of the city,
presided over by Mayor Pratt.


The further fact developed today
that since the removal of Reuter-
dahl as water commissioner, the
mayor has fallen in line with the
new water move at the chamber
of commerce and appointed the
new water commissioner, F. p.
Weymouth, a member of this COM
mittee, to act in conjunction with
the mayor and three members of
the chamber to again settle the
water question"


In a communication filed at the
city clerks office by the board
of public works the first move is
made in the direction of eliminat-
ing the reservoir planned for the
southeast hill section of the city.


The communication is in the ma
ture of ain apology to the city coun-
ell for the continued delay in start
ing the work of building the reser-
voir, a suggestion that the reser-
voir does not go to the root of the
water question, and that II q reser-
